name: regulation of cytokine production
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of production of a cytokine." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: cytokine production
description: "The appearance of a cytokine due to biosynthesis or secretion following a cellular stimulus, resulting in an increase in its intracellular or extracellular levels." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: apoptotic signaling pathway
description: "The series of molecular signals which triggers the apoptotic death of a cell. The pathway starts with reception of a signal, and ends when the execution phase of apoptosis is triggered." [GOC:mtg_apoptosis]

name: cellular response to endogenous stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus arising within the organism." [GOC:mah]

name: Alcoholic liver disease
description: Alcoholic liver disease

name: cellular response to growth factor stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a growth factor stimulus." [GOC:mah]

name: negative regulation of cytokine production
description: "Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the rate of production of a cytokine." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149]

name: response to growth factor
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a growth factor stimulus." [GOC:BHF, GOC:mah]

name: response to endogenous stimulus
description: "Any process that results in a change in state or activity of a cell or an organism (in terms of movement, secretion, enzyme production, gene expression, etc.) as a result of a stimulus arising within the organism." [GOC:sm]

name: apoptotic process
description: "A programmed cell death process which begins when a cell receives an internal (e.g. DNA damage) or external signal (e.g. an extracellular death ligand), and proceeds through a series of biochemical events (signaling pathway phase) which trigger an execution phase. The execution phase is the last step of an apoptotic process, and is typically characterized by rounding-up of the cell, retraction of pseudopodes, reduction of cellular volume (pyknosis), chromatin condensation, nuclear fragmentation (karyorrhexis), plasma membrane blebbing and fragmentation of the cell into apoptotic bodies. When the execution phase is completed, the cell has died." [GOC:cjm, GOC:dhl, GOC:ecd, GOC:go_curators, GOC:mtg_apoptosis, GOC:tb, ISBN:0198506732, PMID:18846107, PMID:21494263]

name: negative regulation of multicellular organismal process
description: "Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the frequency, rate or extent of an organismal process, the processes pertinent to the function of an organism above the cellular level; includes the integrated processes of tissues and organs." [GOC:ai]

name: Cell adhesion molecule (CAM) interaction
description: Cell adhesion molecule (CAM) interaction

name: regulation of multicellular organismal process
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of a multicellular organismal process, the processes pertinent to the function of a multicellular organism above the cellular level; includes the integrated processes of tissues and organs." [GOC:ai, GOC:dph, GOC:tb]

name: programmed cell death
description: "A process which begins when a cell receives an internal or external signal and activates a series of biochemical events (signaling pathway). The process ends with the death of the cell." [GOC:lr, GOC:mtg_apoptosis]

name: cell death
description: "Any biological process that results in permanent cessation of all vital functions of a cell. A cell should be considered dead when any one of the following molecular or morphological criteria is met: (1) the cell has lost the integrity of its plasma membrane; (2) the cell, including its nucleus, has undergone complete fragmentation into discrete bodies (frequently referred to as apoptotic bodies). The cell corpse (or its fragments) may be engulfed by an adjacent cell in vivo, but engulfment of whole cells should not be considered a strict criteria to define cell death as, under some circumstances, live engulfed cells can be released from phagosomes (see PMID:18045538)." [GOC:mah, GOC:mtg_apoptosis, PMID:25236395]

name: defense response to tumor cell
description: "Reactions triggered in response to the presence of a tumor cell that act to protect the cell or organism." [GOC:add, ISBN:0781735149, PMID:16730260]

name: interleukin-10 production
description: "The appearance of interleukin-10 due to biosynthesis or secretion following a cellular stimulus, resulting in an increase in its intracellular or extracellular levels." [GOC:mah]

name: regulation of interleukin-10 production
description: "Any process that modulates the frequency, rate, or extent of interleukin-10 production." [GOC:mah]

name: positive regulation of multicellular organismal process
description: "Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of an organismal process, any of the processes pertinent to the function of an organism above the cellular level; includes the integrated processes of tissues and organs." [GOC:ai]

name: cell surface receptor signaling pathway
description: "The series of molecular signals initiated by an extracellular ligand binding to a receptor located on the cell surface. The pathway ends with regulation of a downstream cellular process, e.g. transcription." [GOC:signaling]
